steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The text contains an independent clause followed by a noun phrase describing the film (“a quiet black-and-white drama about a family in rural India”), then another independent clause (“Ray's film was quite different”). These structures require a proper sentence boundary.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Option D places a period after “Panchali,” separating the structures into two complete sentences—the first ending with the film title, the second describing the film's contrasting qualities.

Options A, B, and C fail to create proper separation: A introduces a floating article, B and C maintain the improper comma between “India” and the final independent clause.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
